$comment: 15-direction vocabulary taxonomy for landing-page-studio. 5 disjoint families x 3 variants each. Names follow the <aesthetic-anchor> / <exemplar-brand> pattern per scope-decisions.md item 2. Disjointness enforced: T4 planner picks 3 from different families per BrandSpec.

directions.name: Editorial / Linear-Stripe
directions.summary: Magazine-grade typography with dense paragraphs, refined whitespace, and strong vertical rhythm. Ink-on-paper gravitas. Favored by Stripe, media brands, and editorial-first SaaS.
do: Lead with a large, confident typographic headline (no decorative hero image)
Use generous line-height (1.6-1.8) and tight letter-spacing on display type
Separate sections with ruled lines or minimal horizontal dividers
Use a restrained 2-3 color palette anchored in near-black and off-white
Treat images as editorial inserts — captioned, bordered, purposeful
dont: Add full-bleed hero gradients or abstract background art
Use rounded corners on cards (radius max 2px)
Mix more than 2 typefaces
Use icon grids or feature bento tiles
Animate the hero section

directions.name: Editorial / Stripe-Press
directions.summary: Book-publishing aesthetic applied to SaaS. Dense knowledge, editorial hierarchy, serif-led type. Communicates depth and intellectual confidence. High contrast, sparse color.
do: Use a serif display font paired with a readable sans body
Structure sections like book chapters — numbered, titled, nested
Present social proof as pull-quotes with attribution, not logo rows
Keep the palette monochromatic with a single accent ink color
Use grid-aligned photography in grayscale or duotone
dont: Add any animation or motion
Use gradients or glassmorphism
Include bento card grids
Put CTAs above the fold in a hero button — tuck them into body text

directions.name: Editorial / Apple-Newsroom
directions.summary: Clean, high-production editorial with precision typography and disciplined white space. Product photography as editorial object. Single-column narrative flow.
do: Use SF Pro or equivalent geometric sans at large display sizes
Lead each section with a single large image or video still, captioned
Use narrow measure (60-70ch) for body text columns
Employ generous vertical spacing between sections (80-120px)
Suppress navigation — minimal sticky header only
dont: Use card-based layouts or grids in the main flow
Add social media widgets or external embeds
Use more than one accent color
Add hover animations on body text or images

directions.name: Bento / Notion-Vercel
directions.summary: Modular card grid with asymmetric tiles, soft borders, and mixed content types per cell. The canonical modern SaaS layout — dashboard-like, feature-rich, scannable.
do: Use a 12-column CSS grid with cards of varying widths (2-col, 4-col, 6-col)
Give each bento cell a clear single purpose (metric, feature, quote, image)
Use soft borders (1px, 10-16px radius) and subtle box shadows
Apply a light background with dark text; one accent color for interactive cells
Include a live-data widget cell (even if mocked) to signal product depth
dont: Make all cells the same size — asymmetry is the point
Use serif type in cells (sans only)
Add full-bleed background images
Use heavy drop shadows or gradients

directions.name: Bento / Vercel-Edge
directions.summary: Dark-mode bento with glowing accent borders and code-adjacent aesthetics. Developer-facing. Terminal-inspired cells alongside feature tiles. Light emitted from within cards.
do: Use a near-black (#0a0a0a or #111) background with white text
Apply glow effects on card borders using box-shadow with accent color
Include a code snippet cell with syntax highlighting
Use monospace type for technical cells, geometric sans for narrative
Animate a single cell (e.g. a terminal typing animation) — keep the rest static
dont: Use light-mode or mixed-mode backgrounds
Add stock photography
Make CTAs look like marketing buttons — style as CLI commands or terminal prompts

directions.name: Bento / Linear-Cards
directions.summary: Precision bento with tight grid, exact spacing, and product UI screenshots as card content. Optimized for issue-trackers, project tools, and B2B productivity SaaS.
do: Use 8px grid — all spacing is multiples of 8
Fill cells with actual UI crops or wireframe mockups (not illustrations)
Apply a neutral palette with one electric accent (neon blue, acid green)
Use SF Mono or JetBrains Mono for UI labels inside card screenshots
Keep cards borderless but differentiated by background shade
dont: Use illustration or icon-heavy cells
Add gradients inside cells
Mix layout densities — keep all cells uniformly compact

directions.name: Hero-art / Lovable-Anthropic
directions.summary: Large hero illustration or abstract art occupies the viewport above the fold. Generous vertical rhythm below. Soft gradients, friendly colors, and human warmth. Favored by AI and creative tools.
do: Dedicate 60-80% of the viewport to the hero visual (illustration, gradient blob, or abstract art)
Use a pastel or soft gradient palette (lavender, peach, warm coral)
Apply large display type (80-120px) with tight tracking overlaid on the hero
Transition to text-focused sections below the fold with generous spacing
Use organic shapes for section dividers (SVG waves or gentle curves)
dont: Use a bento or card-based hero
Choose a dark background (keep it light and airy)
Add hard-edged borders or structured grids in the hero zone
Use stock photography

directions.name: Hero-art / Anthropic-Claude
directions.summary: Centered hero with abstract generative art and a commanding single-line headline. Pure white or off-white base. Premium feel through negative space and restraint.
do: Center everything — headline, subhead, CTA — no left-aligned layouts in the hero
Use a single abstract image or SVG art piece as the visual anchor (not photography)
Apply a warm off-white (#fafaf8 or similar) as the page background
Use a heavy serif or slab serif for the hero headline
Keep the CTA row to one primary + one ghost button, centered
dont: Use logo clouds or testimonial carousels above the fold
Add background video
Use more than 2 colors in the hero zone
Apply grid-based layout to the hero

directions.name: Hero-art / Vercel-AI
directions.summary: Dark hero with subtle animated mesh gradient or particle field. Text floats over a living background. Developer-friendly but polished. Bridges the art and tech aesthetics.
do: Use a dark hero (#0d0d0d) with a subtle animated gradient mesh (CSS or canvas)
Keep animation subtle — movement should feel ambient, not distracting
Use large white or near-white headline type with strong contrast
Surface a secondary featured stat or quote below the hero in a lighter section
Include a product demo GIF or still in the first scroll-stop section
dont: Make the animation loop distracting (keep opacity < 0.4 for the gradient layer)
Use serif type in the hero
Add a bento or card layout in the hero zone

directions.name: Brutalist / Are.na
directions.summary: Raw geometry, sans-serif weights, exposed structure. No ornament — function as form. Thick borders, grid lines, and strict 2-3 color palettes. Favored by design studios and research-forward products.
do: Use thick borders (3-6px solid) on containers, buttons, and section dividers
Expose grid lines and gutters as a deliberate design element
Stick to 2-3 colors total: near-black, off-white, one accent (often red or yellow)
Use monospace or geometric sans for ALL type (body and display)
Align everything strictly to an 8px or 12px grid
dont: Use any border-radius (radius must be 0)
Soften with gradients or drop shadows
Add icons as decoration (only functional icons)
Use photography (illustration or pure type only)

directions.name: Brutalist / Bold-Sans
directions.summary: Oversized geometric sans headlines, ultra-high contrast, hard-cut layout sections. Aggressive typographic rhythm. Brand statement over product explanation.
do: Use display type at 120-200px — type IS the hero
Apply ultra-high contrast: pure black on pure white, or vice versa
Break layout with asymmetric column splits (70/30, 80/20)
Use uppercase for all headings
Treat section breaks as hard cuts — no transition, no wave dividers
dont: Use any gradient or color beyond the 2-color palette
Apply rounded corners anywhere
Use more than one typeface family

directions.name: Brutalist / Mono-Grid
directions.summary: Monospace type throughout, rigid grid, exposed column gutters. Evokes terminal / spreadsheet aesthetics. Developer-native, unpolished by design.
do: Use monospace type for ALL text (JetBrains Mono, Fira Mono, or Courier)
Expose the grid with thin 1px lines — make the structure visible
Use tabular data layouts even for non-tabular content (fake spreadsheet rows)
Keep all content left-aligned with strict 4px or 8px indent steps
Use a raw HTML aesthetic: no decorative framing, no card shadows
dont: Use sans or serif type for any element
Add visual decoration (icons, images, gradients)
Center any text or element

directions.name: Eastern-typographic / Kenya-Hara
directions.summary: Vertical and horizontal type rhythm inspired by Japanese graphic design tradition. Generous negative space, restrained palette (near-white, ink black, one accent). The absence of visual noise IS the design.
do: Use generous negative space — content should feel like it is breathing
Apply a near-white background (#f9f7f4 or equivalent) with ink-dark type
Use a single accent color sparingly (one element per section maximum)
Alternate between horizontal and vertical typographic rhythms across sections
Treat empty space as a structural element — not a gap to fill
dont: Add decorative elements, illustrations, or background textures
Use more than 3 colors total
Apply drop shadows or gradients
Fill sections edge-to-edge — always leave breathing room

directions.name: Eastern-typographic / Muji-Editorial
directions.summary: Warm neutrals, understated typography, and craft-material textures. The paper-and-linen aesthetic applied to digital. Communicates quality through restraint.
do: Use warm neutral backgrounds (linen, cream, warm gray: #f5f0e8 range)
Apply a serif typeface for headings (gentle, not slab — think Cormorant or Libre Baskerville)
Use photography of physical materials, craft objects, or hands-on process
Keep sections narrow-width with large margins on both sides
Use light rule lines and hairline borders as the only structural decoration
dont: Use saturated or primary colors
Add digital-looking gradients or glassmorphism
Use icon grids or bento tiles
Apply heavy typography weights (max 500 weight)

directions.name: Eastern-typographic / Tokyo-Type
directions.summary: High-density typography inspired by Tokyo street typography and neon signage. Mixed scale contrast: large kanji-inspired display type alongside small dense body. Bold + restrained alternation.
do: Apply extreme scale contrast: 120px+ display type next to 12px body text
Use a high-contrast dark palette with 1-2 neon accent colors (electric blue, hot pink)
Alternate between full-bleed sections and tightly constrained text columns
Use a condensed sans or display typeface that references East Asian proportions
Apply red rule lines as typographic markers (inspired by hanko stamps)
dont: Use soft or muted colors — this direction is high-contrast, not zen
Apply rounded corners or soft shapes
Mix more than 3 colors (background, type, one accent)
